entry.title: Re: New Interactive E-Learning for SAP EarlyWatch Alert – Available Now!

Before accessing the link, please note that a one-time registration is required for the SAP Learning Hub, edition for SAP Enterprise Support. You can find a detailed, step-by-step registration guide  here.)

Kindly retry after the one-time registration  for the SAP Learning Hub.

Regards,

Rozyta

entry.title: Re: Tax details are deleted automatically in BP identific...

entry.title: Re: Fiori App 4988 - Team Member Authorization Status
entry.content: Hi DominikK we have found this function, SAP_BC_USR_731_PFCG_REMOTE, and we assigned it to category team in configuration Assign Functions to Template Roles. After that, worked

entry.title: Re: Error while determining ref.mov.type for WM via Table 156S: 161/X/X/ /L/ /
entry.content:
Hello team,

I'm also facing the same issue while creating IBD against normal PO and IBD at receiving plant in case of intra STO for LEIH material type. can you please share the what entry needs to be maintained so that it would work.

and we are not using WM, we are having embedded EWM, so is this entry needed if we are using EWM.

entry.title: Re: Cloud Integration - How to Setup Secure HTTP Inbound Connection with Client Certificates
entry.content:
Hi Mandy,

I'm currently working on a client certificate authentication setup using the SOAP connector (instead of the Process Direct adapter) within a standard integration.  SAP BTP Neo Cockpit.

Here’s a summary of what I’ve done so far:

  • Downloaded the certificate from the URL:
    https://<tenant>-iflmap.<data center>.hana.ondemand.com

  • Uploaded the certificate under Certificate-to-User Mappings. Used the appropriate user name in the Private Key Alias while configuring the SOAP adapter (screenshot attached).

  • My user is already assigned the ESBMessaging.send role in the BTP Neo cockpit.

    However, I'm encountering the following error during execution:

     

     
    org.apache.camel.CamelExchangeException: Multicast processing failed for number 1. Exchange[4F3BA7930FB1482-0000000000000003]. Caused by: [org.apache.camel.CamelExchangeException - Multicast processing failed for number 1. Exchange[4F3BA7930FB1482-0000000000000005]. Caused by: [org.apache.cxf.interceptor.Fault - Could not send Message.]], cause: org.apache.cxf.transport.http.HTTPException: HTTP response '401: Unauthorized' when communicating with https://l0810-iflmap.hcisbp.us2.hana.ondemand.com/cxf/CustomMapping_Test
     

    Could you please advise on how to resolve this issue?

    This is quite urgent, so a quick response would be greatly appreciated.

    Best regards,
    Durga Kalyan

  •

entry.title: Re: SAP Community Escape House for July – SAP Cloud Application Event Hub ⚽️ 🏆 🏟️
entry.content: Escaped this room as well 💪

entry.title: Re: What’s new in Mobile development kit client 25.6
entry.content:
@kaimarkuslueer 

Will push notification images be generated as well? >> No. AppImages are for generating app launcher and splash icons only.

is there a good way to combine the old way with "AppImages"? >> I didn't understand the ask here. Could you elaborate on this?

entry.title: Derived role status in GRC BRM is not same as Master role after the approval step is completed
entry.content:
Dear SAP community,

The status of the master role and the derived roles is inconsistent. The master role is set to Completed after approval; however, the derived roles are not reflecting the same Completed status.Could you please advise what is the issue here.

Thanks.

entry.title: Re: SAP Datasphere Security & Data Access Controls Overview
entry.content:
@Matthew_Shaw : Hello Matthew, is there any way that the permission table used for DAC can be populated either from SAML attributes or by an access to the application user table in SAP Datasphere? 

A manual maintenance of row-level-security (direct entry, csv-upload, etc.) is not very compliant in our case, so we are looking for possibilities to have an automatic derivation of the permission table (without using BW or S4).

Thanks a lot!

entry.title: Re: SAP Datasphere Security & Data Access Controls Overview
entry.content:
@oroerig Did you check out the following blog ?

https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blog-posts-by-sap/integrate-sap-s-4hana-authorizations-into-sap-datasphere/bc-p/14145966/

entry.title: Asset accounting early payment discount
entry.content:
Hi all,
When you post an asset invoice with early payment discount and meet the payment term, the discount is posted to a separate GL account.
Is there any (default) possibility to post the early payment discount for these invoices against the asset? Other invoices with early payment discount should post as default process.

So for example:
Invoice credit 1000
asset debit 1000

At payment:
invoice debit 1000
Bank credit 970
Early payment discount credit 30

Manual reposting of the achieved discount to the asset is not preferred.

I hope the question is clear.

Thanks.
FIN Asset Accounting

entry.title: SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, Public Sector
Automatic notification when Job Requisition is set to On Hold
entry.content:
Dear SAP expert team,

I would like to have your recommendation on the best practices due to the below request:

Requirement = "When a Job Requisition changes the status to ´on-hold´ (mainly due to lack of internal feedback from stakeholders) | Notifications should be automatically triggered to HRBP (Job Requisition´s initiator) Hiring Manager Candidates".

What is your recommendation to apply the solution to this request, considering we do not have, for example, the enablement of the automatic trigger for "on hold Jobe Requisition" at the Recruiting Email Triggers feature?

Thank you.

entry.title: Re: Story Report Missing Data
entry.content: Hi, the Objective plan name is shown for users who have objectives. However, when filtering for users without objectives, no Objective plan is displayed for them.

entry.title: Re: Establishing Inbound Connection from AWS to HANA Clou...
entry.content:
Hello Alex,
Thank you for your response. We already have the connection working using AWS PrivateLink, but we also want to try out the SAP Cloud Connector.
Hello Ulrich,
Thank you for the suggestion to use a Service Channel. We looked in our Cloud Connector and in the "Service Channels" section located under the On-Premise to Cloud menu, when we clicked to add a new channel there, the 'Database' type is not available in the dropdown list(Attached image). The only two options I can see are: ABAP Cloud System K8s Cluster. Is there a reason the 'Database' option might not be appearing in this list? We are using the Cloud Connector version 2.18.1  Any clarification you can provide would be greatly appreciated.
Thanks again!
Shashank Mishra

entry.title: Re: SAP Datasphere Security & Data Access Controls Overview
entry.content:
@Kishore50 Yes I checked that, but the prerequisite is that all users are available in an S4 system. But those users are not available in any S4 system at all.

Ideal scenario would be, if I can either read SAML Attributes for DAC as well or to read the table where Application users are stored, then I can do it with Standard/Scope Role assignment as well.

entry.title: Re: Blog incorrectly displayed in Q&A section
entry.content: Hi @Lakshmipathi - thank you for reporting this. I will reach out the author to have him submit a blog post instead. (this is not a technical issue, but instead a blog post published as a question, we see it quite often)

entry.title: How to check whether a backup is scheduled with IQ events.
entry.content:
Hi Team,

Could we determine how backups are being executed on the Sybase IQ server?
We have information about the backup procedure and can confirm that it is running, with backup files being stored on the local file system.
We also checked the crontab but did not find any scheduled jobs related to backups.

Could you please confirm if there is any other way to identify the source or method from which the backups are being initiated? 

Thanks,


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: How to check whether a backup is scheduled with IQ ev...
entry.content:
Dear Customer,
since you have check the ways outside I server the backup could have been scheduled here is how to check whether it is scheduled from inside SAP IQ server.

IQ server has the possibility of scheduling JOBS using "IQ events".
there are 2 views that bring an accurate information about it:

  • SYSEVENT System View | SAP Help Portal
  • SYSSCHEDULE System View | SAP Help Portal

You can get the full details about querying those views to get as much details as you wish by following the steps of the KBA## 3465884 -How to check the scheduled event details - SAP IQ

entry.title: Re: BO Interface APIs : An alternative to BAPIs and traditional FMs
entry.content: Hi Sudhan, very nice blog.  Is it possible to call these BO's via RFC?

entry.title: Pb extraction de fichier excel dans SAP logon
entry.content:
Bonjour 

J'essaie d'extraire un fichier Excel sur la liste des anniversaires dans le reporting, mais je reçois ce message : Windows ne trouve pas 'C:\Users\CLAVER~1.NGO\AppData\Local\Kingsoft\WPSOFF~1\1220~1.219\office6\wps.exe/prometheus/ et'. Vérifiez que vous avez entré le nom correct, puis réessayez.

entry.title: SAP Fiori development newsletter July 2025 (issue #35)
entry.content:
Summer is here, bringing hot weather across the U.S. and around the world. SAP is also turning up the temperature with hot new AI-powered innovations, many of which were introduced at Sapphire (see article below). Even more new stuff is planned for TechEd and Devtoberfest in the fall.

For ongoing updates, I encourage you to join our monthly SAP Fiori development roundtable. You can meet online with fellow developers and the SAP engineers who build our professional development tools. Register online or email me for an invitation. Our next meeting is Wednesday, July 9, 2025. 

If you prefer to receive this in your inbox, please subscribe to the SAP Fiori development newsletter.

Development News

Sapphire had great innovations, if you are on public cloud

There were lots of announcements about new innovations for both developers and business users. The question for many customers is when their systems will be current enough to consume them. Read about the two parallel universes – one filled with AI in the cloud and the other grounded in legacy code.
https://community.sap.com/t5/enterprise-resource-planning-blog-posts-by-sap/parallel-universes-the-dual-nature-of-ux-and-ai-among-sap-customers-at/ba-p/14113975

SAP Fiori tools 2506 now available

The 2506 release of SAP Fiori tools includes enhancements that make full-stack development with SAP Fiori elements and RAP even more seamless—from initial app creation to UI adaptation and low-code configuration.
https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blog-posts-by-sap/sap-fiori-tools-2506-update-open-source-faster-app-creation-adaptation/ba-p/14131156

AI-powered project accelerator

The AI-powered project accelerator from SAP Fiori tools automates multiple steps, including creating a data model, preparing mock data, building an OData service, generating an SAP Fiori app and adding the necessary annotations. See how to set up a trial account and try it for yourself.
https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blog-posts-by-sap/from-idea-to-app-using-ai-powered-project-accelerator-to-develop-sap-fiori/ba-p/14095300

Introducing SAPUI5 version preview in SAP Build Work Zone

We have introduced a new option to preview an upcoming SAPUI5 version for SAP Build Work Zone. This capability aims to provide a controlled environment for evaluating SAPUI5 updates in SAP Build Work Zone, thereby avoiding immediate changes in production environments.
https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blog-posts-by-sap/introducing-sapui5-version-preview-in-sap-build-work-zone/ba-p/14117996

Explore SAPUI5 1.136.x-legacy-free for future-proof UI5 apps

SAPUI5 1.136.x-legacy-free deprecated code removed is now available via cloud access. Test your code for future-proof best practices and provide early feedback to the UI5 team.
https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blog-posts-by-sap/explore-sapui5-openui5-1-136-x-legacy-free/ba-p/14103271

SAPUI5 is leading the way in accessibility with WCAG 2.2

Accessibility is key to using SAP's business solutions. Adopting the latest standards ensures that solutions are inclusive and meet the diverse needs of users globally. When creating apps with SAP Build and SAPUI5, developers can leverage the latest Web Content Accessibility Guidelines (WCAG).
https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blog-posts-by-sap/sapui5-is-leading-the-way-in-accessibility-with-wcag-2-2/ba-p/14101596

SAP Fiori Application Generator available in open source

The SAP Fiori tools team is delighted to announce that the underlying code for the SAP Fiori Application Generator has been moved entirely to open source. This has been a long journey...
https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blog-posts-by-sap/sap-fiori-application-generator-available-in-open-source/ba-p/14109021

New SAP Design System portal for SAP Fiori design guidelines

The SAP Design System portal contains everything about the design system, including comprehensive new UI Kits for designers in Figma.
https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blog-posts-by-sap/new-sap-design-system-portal-for-sap-fiori-design-guidelines-and-new-ui/ba-p/14109558

Quickly generate and deploy SAP Fiori apps from ABAP Development Tools for Eclipse

The new quick SAP Fiori application generator lets you generate an application based on SAP Fiori elements annotations and deploy the SAP Fiori UI with only a few clicks from the service binding editor in ADT.
https://community.sap.com/t5/application-development-and-automation-blog-posts/quickly-generate-and-deploy-sap-fiori-apps-from-abap-development-tools-for/ba-p/14116822

Events

SAP Fiori Innovation Day Bangalore 

The biggest SAP Fiori Innovation Day ever!
This summary highlights the key sessions, insights from our presentations, and the remarkable contributions from our local development community.
https://community.sap.com/t5/enterprise-resource-planning-blog-posts-by-sap/sap-fiori-innovation-day-bangalore-connection-gratitude-ai-and-local/ba-p/14129580

SAP Fiori Innovation Day Dallas

  •              October 17 as part of the ASUG chapter meeting
  •              Registration link opening soon - mark your calendar now

SAP Fiori Innovation Day Paris

  •              December 10
  •              Registration link opening soon - mark your calendar now

All our SAP Fiori Innovation Day events are open to customers.
If you are a partner, please let me know if you are interested in sponsoring in one or both of our locations.
I hope you can join us.

SAP TechEd

Registration is now open for both the in-person event in Berlin and the virtual event. Here are details on what you can expect.
https://community.sap.com/t5/sap-teched-blog-posts/sap-teched-2025-registration-is-live-check-out-our-brand-new-hybrid/ba-p/14107573

Back issues from the past year

SAP FIori development newsletter May 2025 (Issue #34)

SAP Fiori development newsletter March 2025 (Issue #33)

SAP Fiori development newsletter January 2025 (issue #32)

SAP Fiori development newsletter November 2024 (issue #31)

SAP Fiori development newsletter September 2024 (issue #30)

SAP Fiori development newsletter July 2024 (issue #29)

entry.title: Re: Story Report Missing Data
entry.content: @Punita this is absolutely expected, as they have no goals yet

entry.title: BADI : /SCWM/EX_CORE_PTS_NBIN_NRM To allow mixed storage of different batches in same bin
entry.content:
Dears ,
I have scenario that my customer need receive by Product not HU so the customer needs mixed storage of different batches with same product in same bin, i found SAP NOTE : 2409948  for that issue and they recommend use BADI : /SCWM/EX_CORE_PTS_NBIN_NRM
I need guide to implement this BADI  
Thanks

entry.title: Re: Landed Costs in SAP Business ByDesign - Inbound Process (Moving average & capitalization)
entry.content:
hello stefan,

i had an issue when i want to create the Landed Cost Clearing, i already checked the procedure is same as you even when i checked the report from Landed Cost - Monitor and Set Clearing Status for Goods Receipts the status is "To be Cleared" but when i want to create the landed cost clearing, the error pops up like this 

 

Landed Cost - Monitor and Set Clearing Status for Goods Receipt

Best Regards,

Farrel

entry.title: Re: Tutorial for Project Management Module in SAP Business One
entry.content:
Hello Jitin,

Yes, that document looks good and extensive enough for my purpose.  Thank you very much.

Regards, Koichi

entry.title: Re: For certain materials, orders should only be allowed...
entry.content:
Hello @Mahavir 

in the product master there is a standard field "delivery unit". Only exact multiples of this number can be delivered. E.g. in the case of a delivery unit of 30 pieces, 30, 60, 90, and so on pieces can be delivered, but not, however, 20 or 100 pieces.

In app "Manage Product Master Data" you maintain this field per distribution chain:

When using this material in a sales order and you maintain an order quantity which is not a multiple of the delivery unit, the system shows a warning.

 

I hope this is what you are looking for.

Regards, Doreen

entry.title: Re: Enhanement on BP REL Handler class : MDG
entry.content:
Hi Karthik,

I will suggest you to try out these customizations instead of writing an enhancement code
1) To hide the required relationship category from MDG UI, go to transaction BUBA and set the differentiation type with a value > 0
2776784 - MDG-BP/C/S: Some Relationship category cannot be selected from MDG UI - SAP for Me
2) To hide the required relationship category from the backend maintenance transactions like BP, go to the view V_TB901 and set hide under General data.

Regards,
Arun Raj.

entry.title: I want to create a button that will sort a dimension in a table by the highest to lowest value
entry.content:
I want to create a button that will sort a dimension in a table by the highest to lowest value. In my table i have a date dimension in the column.

Table_1.sortByValue({[Alias.MeasureDimension]:"00O2TM15XD8XM22WI2SLFCF89"},SortOrder.Descending,Direction.Vertical);

I have used the above script and this has worked in the past - but now is not working - i am gettin the message " Sort is removed because it no longer valide"    (not to sure if its something to do with the date dimension). 

Can anyone help with this?

 

@Lothar_Kder  @N1kh1l - do you have any idea on this? I know you have helped me in the past.

 

Thanks 

 

Peers

entry.title: Re: Seamless planning integration between SAP Analytics Cloud and SAP Datasphere - product FAQ
entry.content:
@Max_Gander , Thanks for putting together the FAQs. I have a question on choosing the Datasphere space for storage, is it only HANA space or Data lake space(HDLF) of DSP also can be chosen as storage space?

Thanks

Sreekanth

entry.content: @Riyaz_abdul please validate the original issue with recent release of MDK client (25.6) and let us know if you still experience the same problem.

entry.title: Re: Validating the Valuation Class in Material Master MM01
entry.content: Thanks for the comment, it seems to me that ABAP is the only solution to achieve the requirement, another query would be possible to place the field (BKLAS) only visualization in MM01

entry.title: Re: Pb extraction de fichier excel dans SAP logon
entry.content:
Corrigez ce chemin, qui est un mélange de « \ » et « / » (Windows vs Unix ?).

  • Si besoin, ce n'est pas votre saisie, alors vérifiez la définition du chemin physique dans FILE.

entry.title: Re: Page Navigation from one page to multiple pages in a story with filtered data in navigated page

entry.title: Re: Multiple Country Activation in CBC
entry.content: Thanks @Sandeep_Kumar for your response. Do you mean that after activating 5 countries, the org structure should be created without confirmation before activating additional countries? I want to clarify that the statement in the original question can be followed to the letter, which is to select 5 countries, select complete activity button, then select additional countries, all without creating any org structure.

entry.title: Re: Crystal Reports 2020 and 2025 - 32-bit Connections?

entry.title: Request for Enabling Periodic Billing in Sales Order – SAP Public Cloud
entry.content:
Dear SAP Expert,

We are currently working on creating a Sales Order with Periodic Billing in the SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud. However, we are facing the following issue:

The Periodic Billing option is not available.

The system is defaulting to Milestone Billing, and we are unable to change the billing plan type to Periodic.

We request your assistance in this matter. If there is a configuration, workaround, or standard SAP solution available to enable Periodic Billing in Public Cloud, kindly share the details with us.

Looking forward to your support.

entry.title: ABAP Rest API URL 302 Temporary Redirect Problems
entry.content:
Hi,

I created a REST API on SICF using the IF_HTTP_EXTENSION interface in ABAP(http://xx1/zzcustomapi?sap_client=xxx). Since the external customer cannot directly open this service, we redirected the request to a new URL (http://yy1/zzcustomapi?sap_client=xxx) and used HTTP 302 Temporary Redirect for this purpose.

When the external customer makes a request to the URL http://yy1/..., the REST class on the SAP side works, but the request body content is not coming—the body is empty.

What is the reason for the request body data being empty? Is 302 management incorrect or should we proceed with web dispatcher? Can you help?

Kind regards, aatan.

entry.title: Re: Optimizing Your SAP S/4HANA Landscape with a Best Practice Role Concept
entry.content:
Hi Markus,

Is this a paid service? Is there any partner learning journey for this?

entry.title: Plateau Report Designer LMS - Error dataset at initial software start
entry.content:
Hello everyone,

I'd like to report some issues we're facing regarding Plateau Report Designer for a custom report for LMS SuccessFactors.

When we import the file, even before entering the Query of the dataset, an error appears:

"GetConnection: url and jnd1NameUrl are both of null value."

"java.lang.NullPointerException: GetConnection: url and jndiNameUrl are both of null value."

Then when we add something to the Query another one follows:

org.eclipse.birt.data.engine.core.DataException: Failed to prepare the query execution for the data set: LearningNeedsDataSet

We need to add to the report custom column from User, 30 and 40.
Any ideas?

Best regards,

DP

Hi @SreekanthSurampally 

It is always a HANA space.

BR
Max

entry.title: Re: Output Parameter Determination for Production Order O...

entry.title: Re: Write off approval by standard WS21000102 FICA_WRTOFF and event 5512
entry.content: Hi - i dont understand what you are trying to do and which is not already covered by the standard workflow. Please elaborate more on your expected results vs what happens in standard.

entry.title: Re: Candidates receiving an error when trying to access Offer Letter from DocuSign
entry.content:
Hi All - The details are fuzzy but we were able to resolve our DocuSign issue.
We worked with our DocuSign Administrator and went through the configuration document to enable again and this resolved the issue.

Configuring Online Offers with eSignature in Manage Recruiting Settings | SAP Help Portal

Hopefully this resolves your issue as well.

entry.title: Re: Does "Insurance Values" exist in FI/AA - SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud?
entry.content: Dear all, Is this statement still valid or is there any possibility to implement insurance values in S/4HANA public cloud?

entry.title: Discover the All-New Summary Redesign in the Customer Insights Dashboard
entry.content:
We are excited to announce a significant redesign of the Summary area in the Customer Insights Dashboard within SAP for Me! This update is a direct response to user feedback from customers, internal users, and our management team, all with the goal of making your reporting experience clearer and more actionable.

Why the Change?

As Customer Insights dashboard has grown to support more solutions, products, and analytics, the original Summary area became crowded. With each section offering its own summary indicators (or KPI cards), the display could become quite busy. This made it challenging for users to quickly find the indicators relevant to their area of interest, as the previous order simply followed the order of the data sections. For example, Product Portfolio KPIs came first, followed by Entitlements, then Systems, Cases, and so on—spreading over multiple rows and requiring more effort to focus on a specific topic.

What’s New?

Based on your feedback, we’ve grouped the summary indicator cards into dedicated areas that reflect key domains within your SAP landscape. The new structure makes it much easier to recognize which KPIs relate to which part of your SAP solution portfolio and lets you focus what matters most to you.

The updated Summary area now features groups such as:

  • Product Portfolio and Adoption Overview
  • System Health Overview
  • Case Management Overview
  • Support Entitlements and Services

This reorganization helps you to navigate more intuitively and focus quickly on the topics relevant to your needs.

Get Started

Explore the redesigned Summary area today to experience how these changes make your analytics more intuitive and your daily work more productive. Click any KPI card to dive deeper into the details, and customize your Summary with filters or personalization options to match your unique focus.

We hope you find the new Summary area helpful, and we’re excited to keep improving Customer Insights dashboard with your input!

Stay updated by subscribing to this blog and visiting the SAP Support Portal / Customer Insights.

SAP for Me is made for you!

entry.title: Re: Return of Inventory in Transit
entry.content: Hello, I understand, so you can just use in sending plant the reverse movement and document for the amount that didn't arrived for the receiving plant and deal with it there move to scrap for example. Did you try that? Or is there something else that you need from the business point of view?

entry.title: Checklist to connect SAP Fieldglass and SAP Task Center
entry.content:
SAP Task Center helps you integrate tasks into a central solution. The service provides a single-entry point for end users to access all their assigned tasks, across multiple task providers, such as SAP Fieldglass. In this blog, we outline the steps required to enable the push of task updates from SAP Fieldglass to SAP Task Center. Read on to find out more.

You can also download this information as a one-page checklist here.


Prerequisites & Required Resources

To begin, review the prerequisites on the SAP Help Portal:

  • Check your entitlements and commercial model
  • Prepare your BTP account
  • Integrate with SAP Cloud Identity Services
  • Verify your central entry point for applications

If all prerequisites have been met, ensure the correct resources are engaged to support the setup of this integration:

  • A user with SAP Fieldglass Configuration Manager access
  • A user with SAP BTP Cockpit access


Configuration Steps

  1. Initial Setup: Complete using the Automatic Setup using a booster (recommended) or the Manual Setup

  2. Enable SAP Task Center in SAP Fieldglass: Complete steps described in Enable SAP Task Center in SAP Fieldglass and raise a case with SAP Fieldglass to enable the sso.sapid2uname custom script

  3. Create New Service Instance: Follow step 1 of the Connect SAP Fieldglass and SAP Task Center procedure

  4. Establish Trust with SAP Destination Service: Complete the steps in Establish Trust with SAP Destination Service, raising a case with SAP Fieldglass to upload the certificate in the SAP Fieldglass tenant Truststore

  5. Enable Push of Task Updates: Follow steps under the ‘Procedure’ and ‘Next Steps’ sections on the Manage SAP Task Center to Enable Push of Task Updates SAP Help Portal page
    • Raise a case with SAP Fieldglass to enable SAP_TASK_PUSH_ENABLED in the CompanySysEnv table for your company code

  6. Complete Remaining Setup Steps: Go to the Connect SAP Fieldglass and SAP Task Center page and resume from step 3 (Create API Application Key) to complete all steps

  7. User Upload: Add the required UUID for all relevant Users with the User Upload
    • The UUID column header will need to be added to the upload file
    • A User’s UUID can be found in SAP BTP


Testing & Go Live

To ensure the integration has been successfully setup, testing should be completed and monitoring setup:

  • Create a document in SAP Fieldglass, such as a Job Posting, and confirm the relevant user receives the task notification through SAP Task Center
    • Note: It is recommended to complete testing in a sandbox environment before making changes in Production
  • The connectivity between the systems can be monitored using functionality in SAP Task Center

 

Helpful Resources

  • Download this checklist for step-by-step guidance through the process of enabling the push of task updates from SAP Fieldglass to SAP Task Center.
  • Complete the Integrate Your SAP Fieldglass Tasks Into SAP Task Center mission on SAP Discovery Center.

entry.title: Re: Manage Workflow for Project Billing Requests setup us...
entry.content:
Hello @Haaroon_Daya 

It’s currently not possible to use a BAdI to control workflow triggering in the Manage Workflow for Project Billing Requests app in the S4 Public cloud. SAP has not released a workflow determination BAdI for this scenario.

Recommended Alternative (Standard Setup),

   Create a custom field (e.g. ZeroRatedFlag) in the Custom Fields and Logic app

  • Business Context: Project Billing Request Header
  • Enable it for: UI + Workflow Conditions

Use the field as a condition in Manage Workflow for Project Billing Requests

  • Set workflow to trigger only when the flag  is TRUE

Optional, Use Custom Logic (if available) to default the custom field based on business rules (e.g. tax code).
References

  • Custom Fields and Logic – SAP Help
  • Manage Workflow for Project Billing Requests – SAP Help

With kind regards

Chuma

entry.title: Re: Request for Enabling Periodic Billing in Sales Order...
entry.content: So Periodic Billing Plans for Service Contract Billing are not suitable for your requirements?

entry.title: Re: Creating tile in public cloud S4HANA
entry.content:
Hello, 

Please take a look at SAP documentation regarding creating custom tiles:

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/0f69f8fb28ac4bf48d2b57b9637e81fa/16b1df7ce64b4bef98071fde8e716332.html?locale=en-US

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_MARKETING_CLOUD/e0cd7c1ecf3d4f2f9feb46ec1c5b68fb/16b1df7ce64b4bef98071fde8e716332.html?locale=en-US

Also here is the SAP Note that might help you:

https://me.sap.com/notes/2944583/E

https://me.sap.com/notes/3405003/E

https://me.sap.com/notes/3601210/E

And here short summary from SAP: 

To activate a custom tile, follow these steps:

1. Open the SAP Fiori Launchpad and navigate to the Extensibility Specialist tab.

2. Access the Custom Tiles app.

3. Press the "Add" button and enter a title for the new tile. The ID will be filled automatically.

4. Fill in the required fields, including the URL and select an icon from the drop-down list.

5. Press "Save" and then click on "Assign Catalogs."

6. Search for and select the relevant business catalogs, then press "OK."

7. Mark the catalogs, and the buttons for Add, Remove, Publish, and Revoke will become active.

8. Press "Publish" and confirm by pressing "OK."

The newly created tile will now be visible to all users in the SAP Fiori Launchpad. If you need to remove the tile later, you can select it in the Custom Tiles app, choose "Assign Catalogs," select the catalogs, and then choose "Revoke."

Hope it will help you,

Jagoda

entry.title: Time tracking for tasks in SAP Cloud ALM
entry.content:
I created project, requirements, user stories and also tasks in SAP Cloud ALM. But I don't see possibility for time tracking. I need to monitor also how many hours and in which day a user spent on task.  How can I allow time tracking for tasks for users, please? Does exist some step by step guide for that?
Thank you in advance for any answer.


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Time tracking for tasks in SAP Cloud ALM
entry.content:
SAP Cloud ALM for Implementation does not provide in depth time recording capabilities for Tasks. But SAP Cloud ALM for Implementation provides support for agile project and task management.

The Task Management app does not allow recording granular dates and times for each user contribution to a Task. Instead, the Tasks are assigned to phases, sprints, and milestones. An individual Task can have its own Planned Completion date and duration. Tasks can be assigned story points and effort points for further assessments and analytics. 

For more information on the SAP Cloud ALM for Implementation reporting capabilities and agile workflows, please refer to this documentation from the SAP Help Portal:

  • SAP Cloud ALM for Implementation - Overview
  • SAP Cloud ALM for Implementation - Analytics 
  • SAP Cloud ALM for Implementation - Tasks - Agile Support
  • SAP Cloud ALM for Implementation - Tasks - Tracking Tasks
  • SAP Cloud ALM for Implementation - Tasks - Gantt Chart View

entry.title: Re: Steps to navigate to a canvas/custom page in List Report application
entry.content:
@monicaraj13 :

I had the same issue and I think that It was related to the fact that.I put my xml tags under the Page container. When I removed it, it worked.

 

I have one question though. Does anyone know how to display a footer toolbar to the canvas page?

entry.title: Re: Report Similar To IP19 & IP24 For Unscheduled Mainten...
entry.content:
Hello, 

Try with the View Browser app and look for ready-to-use data views for maintenance plans, items, and orders. Create new report in Custom Analytical Queries app using those views. You connect the data so that the system knows which plans belong to which items and orders. You should be able to choose the fields you want to see in the report, like order type, plant, planner group, start date, cycle, and scheduling period. Add simple formulas to calculate how many maintenance calls should have happened by today or by the end of the scheduling period, and how many orders were actually created. You save and publish the report so others can use it. 

If you'd like to get smoe information about custom queries and CDS Views use this documentation: 

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/0f69f8fb28ac4bf48d2b57b9637e81fa/4ef099d254cd4f8a96df001496f53952.html?locale=en-US

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/0f69f8fb28ac4bf48d2b57b9637e81fa/e30de6eae4d24d70b65996ac8ff88848.html?locale=en-US

Hope it will help you, 

Jagoda

entry.title: Attachment Upload Issue Cloud Foundry
entry.content:
I have created an application with attachment upload using RAP on BTP ABAP environment. When I try to run the entity set using service binding in BTP ABAP environment attachment uploads works fine but when same application is deployed on cloud, we are getting forbidden error while uploading document.

Has anyone faced similar issues?

entry.content:

Hope you will find it useful!

Jagoda

entry.title: SAP Build Work Zone Free Sevice Plan limit reached
entry.content:
Dear SAP Community, 

Following with the restriction known of SAP Build Work Zone free tier service, 3434634 - SAP Build Work Zone Free Sevice Plan limit reached

Anyone know is there application log on checking who are the user accessing Work Zone as an admin from BTP subaccount usage?

 

Regards,

Ben

entry.title: Re: Establishing Inbound Connection from AWS to HANA Clou...
entry.content:
You are right: I just checked it in our SCC, and the "Database" type seems to be available only for "Neo" sub-accounts, not for "Cloud-Foundry" sub-accounts?!

Database connectivity is not my main topic, so I can't say, whether this is only a temporary or a permanent limitation of the CF platform. 😞

entry.title: Basic Etiquette and Tips for Participating in SAP Community
entry.content:
Welcome to SAP Community! As a member, you join an elite group of more than 1 million professionals from around the globe who specialize in SAP solutions, software, and technology. 

Before you begin participating – whether through blogging, asking and answering questions, joining discussions, or any other community activities that appeal to you – we recommend that you take a moment to read this post so you can learn more about how we expect our members to act in SAP Community. 

Acceptable behavior 

What do want from our members?  

It’s simple.  

Be nice to each other. 

In a community of this size, we recognize that our members come from all over the world, representing many different cultures, with a variety of values, beliefs, and educational backgrounds. Therefore, we may not always agree, but we should always act professionally when participating in SAP Community.  

A community can’t thrive in a toxic environment where members bicker, insult one another, and disrespect our rich diversity. 

SAP Community members are professionals who are here to share – and gain – knowledge. As a member, you should support these activities, encourage other members, and treat others with courtesy and consideration.  

When members ask basic questions or publish blog posts with errors, you shouldn’t react harshly. Think like a coach, not a critic. Try to help them by offering advice on how to improve the content. Use comments to point out mistakes politely. By taking this approach, the members can learn, and everyone benefits from a more positive experience.  

Settling disagreements 

We welcome debate in the community – as long as it’s civil. If you disagree with someone’s opinion, share your perspective in comments. If you notice an issue in a post, point it out. You may even opt to take the conversation private via direct messages (if the member has these messages turned on).  

Whichever method you choose for communicating with other members, treat them with the same level of respect you would expect in turn. If they react negatively, try to maintain empathy. We all have bad days, and these affect our moods. Perhaps they feel embarrassed over a mistake. If they seem particularly upset, know that you acted appropriately and that you can choose to stop engaging with them. This decision reflects positively on you, while their poor behavior reflects poorly on them. 

Don’t get drawn into ugly arguments – and don’t feel compelled to involve our moderators. Our moderators focus primarily on activities that violate the Digital Services Act (DSA). They shouldn’t need to mediate petty squabbles. We’re all professionals here, so we should be able to settle differences amicably. When else fails, ignore and move on.  

Healthy debate can foster growth, and we want discussions and differing points of view in our community. If things get too heated, however, take a breath before deciding whether to continue. If someone is being particularly unpleasant or unreasonable, don’t waste your time. There are other awesome SAP Community members worthy of your attention and who would enjoy talking with you. 

One last bit of advice: While English is the official language of SAP Community, many members aren’t native speakers. As a result, the risk of misunderstanding runs high. Therefore, try to avoid English slang and colloquialisms, as they may lead to confusion. Also, keep in mind that you may be reading something from someone who is doing his or her best to express thoughts and feelings in a second language. If someone seems terse, try not to assume the worst as written communication lacks the nuances of vocal tone and facial expressions evident when we speak face to face. Be charitable in your interpretation of an author’s intent. 

In short, wherever possible, members should try to work things out amongst themselves. When all else fails, reconsider your tactics (is it possible you’ve come across as abrasive?) or maybe exit a thread. Moderators should only intervene as a last resort or when clear violations have occurred. 

When to involve moderators 

 As noted above, you shouldn’t contact the moderation team over content that seems incorrect, challenges your ideas, or points out errors in something you posted. Instead, use comments and messages to talk things through with other members. 

A member doesn’t break the rules by telling you that you’re wrong or that he or she disagrees with you. He or she may even publish statements that are demonstrably incorrect (such as coding errors), as long as they don’t harm anyone or an SAP installation. If a member’s post shows a lack of certain knowledge, we encourage other members to provide corrections via comments. Doing so helps the original poster as well as anyone else reading the post.   

A debate or mistake is not grounds for moderator involvement.  

If the discourse is respectful, you shouldn’t notify the moderation team. 

If, however, a member becomes abusive, you should alert the moderators. 

Abusive can be somewhat subjective, though. A criticism, a curt statement, etc., may come across as unfriendly, but it isn’t necessarily abusive. Take a moment to think about it before sending a complaint to the moderation team. 

We aren’t suggesting you should overlook, or attempt to work with, an obviously hostile member. We do not tolerate truly abusive behavior in SAP Community, and we will take action against members and their posts should they create an unsafe environment. If the content contains obscenities or other offensive language, if the member is demeaning or harassing others, if the statements are defamatory or discriminatory, you absolutely should reach out to the moderation team.  (For example, here is how the DSA defines hate speech: "Illegal incitement to violence and hatred based on protected characteristics." Please notify the moderation team immediately when you see this type of content in SAP Community.)

Be professional…and be yourself 

We understand that some members wish to remain anonymous, but when participants share their authentic selves – posting under their actual names, with profile pics showing who they are – they collectively create a true sense of community. By putting a real face to a real name, you invite other members to get to know the real you, making it much easier for us all to connect. Plus, you help combat an experience where people can hide bad behavior behind anonymity. 

You’re under no obligation to create a profile with your real name and a profile pic, but at the very least, when you post content, you should share your knowledge, written in your unique voice, so that your personality shines through. 

Which brings us to artificial intelligence (AI). 

GenAI is here to stay. We know that members use it to clean up – or even generate – content, but they should be honest about it.  

If you use GenAI to assist you when creating content for SAP Community, you must label it as such. For blog posts, you must add the user tag GenAI Assisted Content. For other content types (e.g., answers), you must include this statement: "GenAI was used to help generate this content.” 

We prefer that members show their authentic selves in their profiles – and in their content – wherever and whenever possible. If they don’t, however, they should be transparent with other members by indicating when AI has been involved in content creation.  

In conclusion (tl;dr) 

OK, maybe we used way too many words to explain what can be summarized easily as follows. 

SAP Community members should: 

  • Be kind to one another. 
  • Work together, politely and professionally, to resolve differences. 
  • Involve moderators only when flagrant violations occur. 
  • Feel comfortable participating as themselves. 
  • Be authentic. 
  • Indicate when they’ve used GenAI tools to create content. 

By following these simple guidelines, you’ll do your part to build and maintain a fantastic SAP community that welcomes everyone and provides a rewarding experience for all.

entry.title: Re: Why the expiry date of SF "Certified Professional" in...
entry.content: Hi Amir! Yes, I am. Thank you! Hope you are doing well too 🙂 No update so far. And my professional certificates on Credly show as expired. Best Regards, Isha

entry.title: Inspection characteristics not carrying over to the inspection lot
entry.content:
I'm stumped. I have a part that is assigned to an inspection plan. The inspection plan has several inspection characteristics. When looking at the inspection lot history I see the last 5 or so all have auto usage decision to accept. When I look at these lots in QE09, i see no inspection characteristics recorded.

Here are some key details on each piece of this puzzle:

  • Inspection Plan
    • valid from Jan 2019 until 29999
    • Usage: Goods receipt 5
    • Status Released/approved
    • There is a 100% inspection sampling plan set up on this
    • The part number is in the material assignment list
    • There is one operation with 4 qualitative inspection characteristics on them.
      • I checked one of the Inspection characteristics
        • Shows is a Required Characteristic in the results confirmation section.
        • A sampling procedure is checked for this though.
        • Characteristic is released and valid since early last year
  • Material master for the part
    • inspection type is set up, it has the standard goods receipt inspection type with preferred inspection type checked/active checked
    • Automatic UD is checked
  • Inspection lot information (from QE09)
    • created May 2025
    • assigned to the inspection plan described above
    • accepted by auto UD
    • no inspection characteristic data

does anyone know why the inspection characteristics are not carrying over to the inspection lot?

entry.title: Re: SAP B1: Duplicated Windows in ALT+TAB
entry.content: Hi, I'm facing the same issue. Did you receive any solution or workaroud?. Thanks!!

entry.title: How to enter more than 11 result in Result recording?
entry.content: I have step up single record in Mic indicator. My lot size calculated 20 inspect but when i enter result in QE01. System allow to enter only 11 result against each MIC. I want to enter more than 11 result..

entry.title: Create/add S-user ID in SAP Cloud ALM Tenant
entry.content:
Hello Team,

We are moving to S/4 HANA rise and in order to complete the Quality Gate questionnaire, we need access to the SAP Cloud ALM tenant.

Can SAP add/create an S-user in the SAP Cloud ALM tenant? Or can you please help us to add/create the S-user in the tenant?

Regards


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Create/add S-user ID in SAP Cloud ALM Tenant
entry.content:
S-user accounts available in SAP for Me are stored in the "Default Identity Provider" in the BTP cockpit Trust Configuration page. We strongly discourage our Customers from connecting their SAP Cloud ALM tenant to "Default Identity Provider" and using S-users as their identity provider.

Instead, Customers should connect their SAP Cloud ALM tenant to an SAP Identity Authentication Tenant (IAS). This gives you full control of the user accounts available in their Cloud Services, as well as the authorizations for those accounts. It also ensures that SAP for Me access & auths are kept separate from Cloud Service access & auths. This is why an IAS tenant is either created when provisioning SAP Cloud ALM, or the provisioner selects an existing IAS to connect the tenant to. As a result, User accounts connected to SAP Cloud ALM are completely separate from S-user accounts in SAP for Me.. 

As a result, User accounts and access to SAP Cloud ALM are managed by Customers, not by SAP. SAP is not able to add users to SAP Cloud ALM or to the Identity Providers (e.g. SAP Identity Authentication Service) connected to SAP Cloud ALM. This task is considered a "self-service" task and must be performed by the Admins of the Customer's systems.

Follow the instructions to onboard users in SAP Cloud ALM, and for further explanation about why S-users are not connected to SAP Cloud ALM: 

  • 3121352 - How to create and onboard SAP Cloud ALM Users in your SAP Identity Authentication Service tenant?
  • 3117604 - User cannot logon to the SAP Cloud ALM tenant with their S-User ID or S-user credentials
  • 3365171 - How to get full Admin/IT Contact access for SAP Cloud ALM?

entry.title: Re: Configuration for secure act 2.0 section 109 roll ove...
entry.content: Thank you for the information Sara.. I'm using the WRNG_CALC and letting it calc the pre-tax and then I wrote payroll rules with validation checks on Age and Over the 145,000 limit and then move it to our Post-tax wage type and remove the pre-tax calculated one. So far in test this has working great for all our testing employee scenarios. So if SAP doesn't deliver an appectable solution we plan on using mine that I bulit a few months ago when SAP said they were not going to provide a solution. Thank you, Joe .

entry.title: Re: SAP B1 BY-PRODUCT
entry.content:
Hi @mari_outtaleb96 ,

If you then need to deduct the By-Product cost from the Product, this is exactly SAP's behaviour. Follow what is described in their documentation, and you should have no problems. I did not.

Common Cases in Production Costing | SAP Help Portal

entry.title: Story Report: Objective management
entry.content:
Hi,

I created the story report to get the Objective related data; I have enabled the permission: Data Access: Story for Objective management, do I need to enable Objective plan permission as well?

Br!

entry.title: Multiaction to import data into analytic data models in SAC in a sequence
entry.content:
Hi,

I have created a multiaction in SAC that carries out data imports to data models (analytic). For instance, if I have 3 data models, then step 1 of the multiaction should do data refresh for model A, step 2 of the multiaction should do data refresh for model B and so on. There is a step in the multiaction feature called 'Data Import Step' that can be used to execute a data import job. Please see the screenshot.

On scheduling this multiaction in the calendar of SAC, though the schedule does its job i.e., carry out import jobs in a sequence, when there is a failure of a particular step (or import job), the rest of the jobs/steps are not executed. A step is dependent on the previous step for its execution. Is there a way to overcome this limitation and have the multiaction task run the steps independently?. 

Please let me know.

 

Regards,

entry.title: Re: Handling Field Length Differences in SAP Ariba – SAP ERP including S/4 HANA Integration Using CI
entry.content: You've nailed a challenge that resonates with many. Good one Dev 👍

entry.title: Customers failing to interface with the newly created Industry key
entry.content:
We are on ECC 6.0 

 

We had a requirement to configure a new Industry and Industry Key with the following values:

 

  • G29 – Corporate – Telecom
  • G30 – Corporate – Health

 

After setting up these new configurations, we observed that customers created for ZSLD are failing to interface into SAP. The IDocs are being generated with status 51, and the error message displayed is:

 

"Business partner A-******** not found, correct your entry."

 

However, despite the error, we can confirm that the customer is successfully created and visible in SAP.

entry.title: Re: Change document for user is not updating
entry.content: i also face same issue, but my case i'm using BAPI_USER_CHANGE in RFC connection, the change documents are not updated only for few users but remaining users are updated properly

entry.title: Re: Time type group container/collector - Cross year cumulating counting overtime hours
entry.content:
I created an Improvement Request to solve this issue: https://influence.sap.com/sap/ino/#/idea/351707/?section=sectionDetails

I think it's quite technical, hopefully companies understand the impact of it to their own SF Time Tracking solution. In the beginning it's not so obvious, because in the first year it seems to work correct. But like you describe, the new year is the problem. Any support is highly appreciated.

entry.title: How to print HU label in Work Centre packing for IBD in public cloud
entry.content:
How to print HU label in Work Centre packing for IBD in public cloud?

Do we have option to print the HU label through work center screen in SAP public cloud?

If yes, at what stage its available and how we can do it?

entry.title: Export a list of the favorites SAP Notes and KBAs
entry.content:
Hello SAP,

Can I export a list of favorites SAP Notes and KBAs from expired S-users?


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: ADSO's and Composite Providers are not visible in Ana...
entry.content: You need to disable DisableBWDirectSearch in the Technical Configuration of AFO. Restart AFO and you should find everything.

entry.title: Re: Export a list of the favorites SAP Notes and KBAs
entry.content:
Hello,

It is not possible to export a list of favorites SAP Notes for users whose accounts have expired.
Once an account has expired it is not possible to access the information related to the users, including saved favorites, is typically restricted in accordance with SAP's data privacy policies.

Best regards,
SAP Support

entry.title: Re: Applying different recording dictionary to recording
entry.content:
Unfortunately the Recording Dictionary and its texts are used only during the initial recording session. There's no simple way to apply a new Recording Dictionary to an existing Simulation, except maybe through Rerecording, which is more of a hassle then just recording a new simulation.

I would have your author select the correct Recording Dictionary as the default in their Producer, then record a new simulation with the correct texts. Or, manually update the Demo and Practice text for each incorrectly-recorded Step. Or, use the Edit > Search & Replace Bubble Content function in the Simulation Editor. That can be tricky and can have unintended results. 

Having the author correct their own mistakes helps them remember to do it correctly the next time. 

Shane/Anton/smart people, please correct/clarify as needed.

entry.title: Re: Asset accounting early payment discount
entry.content:
Hi @Danny5 ,

Couple of steps you need to follow:-

1. Transaction type config - Go to t-code AO73 - define transaction type for asset osting. For the relevant transaction type (in this case 100 for external acquisition), ensure 'Net acquisition' is activated.
2. Go to t-code Obb8 - for payment term config - ensure payment term is set to allow discounts and is net-based (not gross).
3. Go to FBKP - go to your asset dcoument type - double click - go to control data and activate tick mark - 'Net document type'

This how your to-be entry should be

Acquistion

Dr Asset 1000
Cr Vendor 1000

Payment

Dr Vendor 1000
Cr Bank 970
Cr Asset 30 (discount reduces APC — acquisition value)

I hope this answer your question.
If you find my response helpful, kindly award a Kudos as small gesture which motivates me to serve you better.
Appreciate your feedback.
Thanks,
Divyesh

entry.title: Re: Configuration of special periods in SAC
entry.content:
Hello Scott,

we created Special Periods exactly as described in your description and in the video.

However, the Special Periods are still displayed in the old hierarchy in the story, even if you add a "_SP" to the Fiscal Year attribute in the Date dimension. It wouldn't make any technical sense for it to work just because of that. Therefore, I assumed that a filter was somehow set in the video, e.g., in the table. So I filtered by year and then changed the hierarchy as shown in the video. It works. But if I change the hierarchy back to the old one, the Special Periods are also displayed because the filter has disappeared from the table.

Is there a way to prevent this?

 

Thanks and best regards

Goran

entry.title: Re: App UI5 in workzone show loading screen never end
entry.content: I have the same problem, I checked the logs of the cloud connector and didn't find anything conclusive, did you manage to solve it?

entry.title: Re: Customers failing to interface with the newly created...
entry.content:
Hi @GuruDath ,

Are you using ZSLD in any of the custom process, any functional module or badi , or any other vlaidation susbstitution which is triggering while system tries to read the value G29/G30. I would ask you to debugg and verify because chances are it may fail while reading the data due to custom logic.

I hope this answer your question.
If you find my response helpful, kindly award a Kudos as small gesture which motivates me to serve you better.
Appreciate your feedback.
Thanks,
Divyesh

entry.title: Re: Configuration of special periods in SAC
entry.content:
We also noticed a bug during testing.

If you switch to User-Managed within the Date dimension and then add entries from the previous year using Generate Additional Member, the range doesn't seem to be updated in the background. I couldn't load data for the newly added year into the data model via an Excel upload. Strangely, it works for future years. I then created a new data model for testing and first extended the range to the previous year before switching to User-Managed. This made the upload of the previous year's data work.

Does this mean that it's no longer possible to change the range to previous years once the Date dimension has been changed to User-Managed? That would be very user-unfriendly.

 

Best regards

Goran

entry.title: Re: Multiaction to import data into analytic data models...
entry.content:
Hi,

It is by design as you also pointed out.  Please see below:

 https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_ANALYTICS_CLOUD/00f68c2e08b941f081002fd3691d86a7/69a370e6cfd84315973101389baacde0.html

In some cases, one of the multi action steps may not run successfully. Changes from the previous steps will still take effect, but the multi action won’t proceed to the following steps.

I don't think there is any setting like "continue on error" etc. Can't think of any standard workaround either other than breaking down the steps into different data actions/ multi actions which needs to run independently. Apparently this will break the sequential order unless you observe the start / finish times and schedule them accordingly.

entry.title: Re: SAP B1: Duplicated Windows in ALT+TAB
entry.content:
Hi @bastik @jsarciniegasg @Attila_Karpati @Peach14 ,

 

Did you all recently moved to Windows 11? That might be causing issue. I would suggest to swtich off snap layout tab feature.
Simply go to settings---system---multitasking. Under alt+tab, set the option to 'open  windows only' or 'don't show tabs'
This stops windows from grouping snapped layout or browser tabs as separate alt+tab entries.

I hope this answer your question.
If you find my response helpful, kindly award a Kudos as small gesture which motivates me to serve you better.
Appreciate your feedback.
Thanks,
Divyesh

entry.title: document management system
entry.content: How do i create a document management system for images

entry.title: Re: Managing Event Volume: Summary Events and Grouping Events in SAP Cloud ALM
entry.content:
Hello Rashmi,

Thank you for the blog, could you please explain the confirm option in actions menu for the alerts.What is the meaning and scope of confirm?


1. The events captured and alerted to recipeints via an email notification in last calculation, would not be considered for next calculation, until some one clicks confirm?

2. For every alert generated, an incident would be created, if create ticket option is enabled? e.g. in the example given by you, two incidents would be generated, first inc for 4 messages and second Inc for 8 messages, as per the groups created?

3. How can we automate the process of retrying the failed messages in Cloud ALM?

Thanks,

Indu Khurana.

entry.title: Re: Mobile Start-Push Notification
entry.content:
Hi @Thomas, thanks, what user identifier attributes that should match on S4 and IAS for Work zone? We have a setup where notifications are correctly pushed to Work zone from S4/H but Push notification on mobiles not working. The SMP logs on Work zone says that notification were requested but users not registered for push notification. Although we could see them registered and even sent them test messages successfully.

Thanks

MK

entry.title: Re: DUPLICATE VENDORS IN PROPOSAL WITH SEPARATE INVOICES
entry.content:
Hi @Nish0301 ,

Check your vendor master set-up. It might have "individual payment" tick/check box option activated. If that dosen;t solve your problem, then check the payment log in F110, it will clearly state the actual reason - e.g. 'Payment cannot be grouped with other items due to differing house bank' or 'Separate payment created due to payment method difference' whatever is the issue it will highlight.

I hope this answer your question.
If you find my response helpful, kindly award a Kudos as small gesture which motivates me to serve you better.
Appreciate your feedback.
Thanks,
Divyesh

entry.title: Re: How to connect to SQL Anywhere database (v17.0.11.790...
entry.content:
The MIN_TLS_VERSION controls the minimum TLS version permitted but the server and client will negotiate the highest version that both support.   The default should permit either 1.3 or 1.2 TLS - older TLS versions would need to be added.

You can set this option on the server and/or the client.  For the client, it is a network protocol option.
